official_sources.label: OpenAI Codex 产品主页
official_sources.scraped_text: OpenAI Codex is OpenAI's flagship AI Coding Agent platform, rebuilt as a multi-agent coding command center. It connects into the ChatGPT ecosystem and provides standalone App (macOS/Windows), CLI, and IDE extensions. Core differentiator: multi-agent parallel streams + Computer Use — Codex can autonomously spawn isolated cloud environments, execute multi-file refactors, run tests, and open PRs without human supervision. Key features: Isolated Work Trees & Diffs (AI generates and tests code in cloud sandbox, human reviews diffs and approves), Memories (long-term project context), Automations (scheduled background tasks like CI/CD fix automation, bulk API migrations, automatic issue triage). Positioned as a 'digital employee' rather than a coding assistant.

official_sources.label: OpenAI Help Center — Codex 使用指南
official_sources.scraped_text: How to use Codex with your ChatGPT plan: Codex App available for download on macOS and Windows. CLI tool for terminal-native workflows. IDE extension for VS Code and JetBrains. Usage counts toward your ChatGPT plan's Agent quota — heavy users on Plus/Pro plans may hit shared rate limits. Pro and Enterprise plans get priority access. Codex can work in 'background mode' where it autonomously monitors repositories, fixes CI/CD failures, and opens automated PRs for dependency updates or deprecated API migrations.

official_sources.label: OpenAI 开发者文档 — Codex
official_sources.scraped_text: OpenAI 官方开发者文档介绍 Codex 的产品能力覆盖本地终端、IDE、桌面应用和云端软件工程任务。Codex 能读取与修改代码、运行命令和测试、审查变更，并通过可复用技能和工具连接外部工作流；云端任务运行在隔离环境中，便于并行委派、检查执行证据和继续迭代。

community_sources.label: Faros AI — 2026 AI Coding Agents 技术评测
community_sources.scraped_text: Faros AI's in-depth evaluation of Codex (2026): 'Codex's multi-agent architecture is the most ambitious — it can run 3-5 parallel worktrees simultaneously, each tackling a different issue. The Computer Use capability means it can actually use a browser to look up documentation, inspect CI logs, and debug by observing runtime behavior — not just static code analysis. Trust score: 4.2/5. Cost concern: heavy usage on ChatGPT Pro plan burns through agent quota quickly. Best for: teams that want an autonomous CI/CD maintainer that works overnight without prompting.'

community_sources.label: Qodo Blog — AI Coding Tools 全景横评
community_sources.scraped_text: Qodo's 15-tool comparison classifies Codex as a 'Native AI IDE + Agent Platform' alongside Cursor and Windsurf. Key note: 'OpenAI entering the agent platform space directly threatens the plugin economy — why buy a third-party agent when the model provider offers one natively?' Pricing analysis: Codex is bundled with ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo) but with strict agent quota limits. Pro ($200/mo) gives more headroom. Enterprise plans offer unlimited agents with data retention controls.

community_sources.label: Hacker News — OpenAI Codex 讨论
community_sources.scraped_text: Developer reactions to Codex relaunch: 'The isolated worktree approach is genius — it's like having a CI pipeline that writes the code before testing it.' 'Computer Use means Codex can actually verify its work by seeing the app run, which is a huge advantage over text-only agents.' Concerns: 'Another OpenAI product that's amazing at first but throttled by quota limits.' 'The ChatGPT integration feels like lock-in — your project context lives in OpenAI's cloud.' Comparison: 'Codex vs Claude Code: Codex has better automation (scheduled tasks, multi-repo), Claude Code has better reasoning on messy legacy codebases.'

community_sources.label: Reddit — Codex 多智能体实测
community_sources.scraped_text: User review: 'I set up Codex Automations to review and fix failing CI tests every morning. It's been running for 2 weeks — 70% of its fixes work without human intervention. The other 30% it correctly flags for review.' Criticism: 'On a Plus plan, I can't run more than 2 concurrent agents and it drains my quota in a day of heavy use.' Enterprise developer: 'Our team switched from Copilot to Codex for the automation features, but kept Copilot for inline completions — they're complementary.' Feature gap: 'No native JetBrains debugger integration yet, which Cursor has.'

metadata.pricing: Bundled with ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo) / Pro ($200/mo) / Enterprise
